warnings: B3 segment toward D-3 (grid_ref_end='?') and B1 (confidence 0.4) still need human zoom review, same as t3

CORRECTED 2026-07-13 (v2): grid master now has TWO dummy grids past grid 3 -- 3'(7.6, wall line) and 3''(8.5, beam extension). B4X reassigned from C-3'/B-3' back to C-3''/B-3'' with span_length_m restored to 1.5 (its original reading), since B4X represents the beam running past the wall to the outer 3'' line, not the wall corner itself.

CORRECTED 2026-07-14 BY USER: all grid_ref_start/grid_ref_end values changed from dash n2otation (e.g. "D-1") to no-dash (e.g. "D1") -- each names a single POINT (one endpoint of the beam), and per the new convention the dash is reserved for actual line/area RANGES along one axis (e.g. "D-C"), not for combining a row letter with a column number into one point label.

REORDERED 2026-07-14 BY USER: elements[] array reordered from element_id-grouping to reading order -- top-to-bottom (row D, then C, then B, then A), left-to-right within each row (grid1, 1', 2, 3, 3'' ...), and horizontal-orientation-before-vertical when two beams share the same starting point (e.g. at D1: B2 D1-D2 horizontal listed before B4 D1-C1 vertical). Slab markers (SO/SI/ST) have no grid_ref so stay at the end, unordered.

CORRECTED 2026-07-14 BY USER (v2, re-ordering rule reversed): the tie-break rule above was WRONG -- it's vertical-before-horizontal, not horizontal-before-vertical, when two beams share the same point (e.g. at D1: B4 D1-C1 vertical now listed BEFORE B2 D1-D2 horizontal). Re-sorted the whole elements[] array under the corrected rule. Also fixed along the way: B2's second segment had been accidentally miskeyed as D'2-D'3 (typo) -- restored to D2-D3, confirmed via source image (row D has exactly two B2 labels, grid1-2 and grid2-3).

RESTRUCTURED 2026-07-14 BY USER: extracted the repeated rebar/dimension spec block (width_mm, height_mm, main_bar, stirrup, additional_bars, concrete_grade, steel_grade, spec_source, spec_confidence_score) out of every elements[] entry into a new top-level `specs` object keyed by element_id (mark) -- each mark's spec was identical across every occurrence of that mark, so repeating the full block per segment was pure duplication (god-object-in-a-row-per-mark). elements[] entries now only carry position (grid_ref_start/end, span) and per-occurrence confidence data. Verified every mark's spec was byte-identical across all its occurrences before extracting (B2 x2, B4 x3, B5 x3, B3 x3, B4X x2 all confirmed identical per mark).

CORRECTED 2026-07-14 BY USER (re-reading beam section detail, e.g. B2 1:20 on หน้า21/S-04): the previous `additional_bars` entry on B2/B4/B5/B3 was mislabeled `position: "บนคาน หยุดที่ L/8"` (top of beam) -- it actually belongs to the BOTTOM reinforcement per the section drawing (2 continuous bottom bars + 1 shorter bar cut off at L/8, all three drawn together at the bottom of the section). Merged into main_bar.bottom.count (B2/B4/B3: 2->3, B5: 3->5) with a note preserving the curtailment detail (which bars run full length vs. stop at L/8) instead of tracking it as a separate additional_bars entry. additional_bars now empty [] for these 4 marks. At the time, B3X/B5X's additional_bars were left untouched pending their own section detail images (see next entry for B3X's resolution; B5X still pending).

CORRECTED 2026-07-14 BY USER (re-reading B3X section detail, 1:20 on หน้า21/S-04): B3X's `additional_bars` entry (2-Ø16mm ล้วงเข้า B3 1.5 ม.) merged into main_bar.**top**.count (2->4, not bottom this time) -- per the section drawing's label order top-to-bottom (top-continuing bar / lap-splice bar / stirrup / bottom-continuing bar), the lap-splice bars sit at the TOP position, unlike B2/B4/B5/B3's curtailed bars which sat at the bottom. main_bar.bottom stays at 2 (both bars simply continuous from B3, no extra bar). additional_bars now empty [] for B3X.

CORRECTED 2026-07-14 (Claude, self-served by reading the source page directly instead of waiting for a cropped screenshot): read หน้า21.png (S-04, all beam sections B1/B2/B3/B3X/B4/B4X/B5/B5X/B6 on one sheet) directly to resolve B5X. B5X's label order matches B3X exactly (top-continuing '2-Ø16mm(ต่อเนื่องจาก B5)' / lap-splice '1-Ø16mm ล้วงเข้า B5 1.00 ม.' / stirrup / bottom-continuing '3-Ø16mm(ต่อเนื่องจาก B5)') -- lap-splice bar merged into main_bar.**top**.count (2->3), main_bar.bottom stays 3 (unchanged). Result is now SYMMETRIC (top3/bottom3) -- the old 'ASYMMETRIC_main_bar_top2_bottom3' flag no longer applies and was removed. Cross-checked B2/B4/B3/B5's existing bottom-merge corrections against this same clearer page read -- all 4 confirmed correct (curtailed bar's label sits between the stirrup label and the bottom-main label in each case, consistent with belonging to the bottom side).

ADDED 2026-07-14 BY USER (from a clearer full-page beam plan image, hน้า19 right panel): 7 previously-missing beam segments added: (1) B2 D'2-D'3 -- confirms D' is a real BEAM line on the right side (grid2-3), not just a left-side architectural partition as previously flagged in the grid master; (2) B4 B1-B2 and (3) B4 B2-B3 -- horizontal tie beams at row B, mirroring the already-known B4 C1-C2/C2-C3 pattern at row C; (4) B2 B1''-A1'' -- vertical beam at dummy grid 1'' between row B and A, resolving an earlier unlocated mark the user asked about (previously described only as "A1''-B1''" with no mark name); (5) B2 C3'-B3' -- vertical beam at the 3' wall line between row C and B; (6) B3 A1''-A3 and (7) B3 A'1''-A'3 -- horizontal tie beams at row A and row A' (carport area south of grid A), grid1''-grid3. All reuse existing specs (B2/B3/B4 -- assumed same cross-section/rebar per mark name; not independently re-verified per new position). Grid master (หน้า00_gridline.json) should be revisited to raise D' confidence given this new beam-level confirmation on the right side -- not yet done.

RESOLVED 2026-07-14 BY USER (previously flagged as an inconsistency): B4X's two segments had drifted to grid_ref_end "C3'"/"B3'" (span 0.6/1.5, inconsistent with each other). Confirmed via grid master (3=7.0, 3'=7.6, 3''=8.5, diff 3-to-3'=0.6) plus the grid master's own documented history (both B4X segments run the full 1.5m to 3'') that BOTH segments should read 3'' with span 1.5 -- fixed both back to C3''/B3'', span 1.5.
